parsed_review.summary:
CP00-896 is a descriptor-only RP29 Commercial Readiness review-closeout + next-handoff pack (risk C, 15 units, RP29.P09.M09.S14-RP29.P09.M10.S08) routing to CP00-897/RP30.P00.M00.S01. Read-only review confirms the pack-level claims are sound and not over-stated: manifest declares production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false (descriptor-only), which is fully supported by implementation, tests, the generated contract, and the no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data boundaries.

Cross-checks passed: (1) COMMERCIAL_CP896_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:456) matches baseline/manifest/live plan for id, risk, unit_count, first/last unit, and next pack/subphase. (2) Section rows M09=COMMERCIAL_REVIEW_PACKET_ROWS.slice(13,20) (7 rows: Closeout criteria..Command rerun) and M10=slice(0,8) (8 rows: Architecture review questions..Risk register) match the manifest unit titles S14-S20 and S01-S08 exactly; deliverable distribution (implementation 9, claude_review 2, security_audit 2, test 1, ui 1) and micro-title distribution (Claude Review Packet 7, Closeout And Next Handoff 8) match prompt, COMMERCIAL_CP896_REQUIREMENTS, and the plan. (3) createReviewPacketSectionRows (registry.js:2390) sets descriptor_only=true and runtime_execution/permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/runtime_receipt_emitted/real_client_data_included/credential_or_secret_included=false, command_rerun_executes=false, claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true; validateCommercialCp896ReviewCloseoutHandoffDescriptor (validators.js:3025) enforces all of these plus descriptor type/source, authority_boundary, Hermes/Claude packet shape, and handoff drift. (4) Authority boundary preserved: Claude packet read_only=true and promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false and Hermes is not embedded in product runtime (deterministic review-packet/receipt pattern only). (5) Pack chain CP895->CP896->CP897 verified in scripts/validate-rp29-commercial-readiness-contract.mjs:200-211; generated contract latest_pack/current_pack=CP00-896 with validation.valid=true, and CP896 capabilities/safety-gates/no-leak guards present at top level; compatibility alias points to the canonical contract with latest_pack_id=CP00-896. (6) model.test.js:1004-1063 asserts coverage, descriptor, contract projection, and alias for CP00-896 consistent with the validators.

CP178+ hardening: this review evidence is schema-valid JSON with all required fields, no auth failure, no empty/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output. The pending-review markers in claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, and adjudication.md are the expected pre-receipt state that this review resolves; they are not defects. Verification was static read-only (Bash not run); command-evidence.json records the targeted validations at exit 0, and the full deterministic validation ladder plus receipt normalization/adjudication update run post-review per the documented per-pack flow. blocks_goal_closeout=false because the pack does not assert runtime or enterprise-trust; those remain explicitly deferred. One observation considered and dismissed: the M10 micro-phase titled 'Closeout And Next Handoff' carries review-question row titles (reused review-packet vocabulary), but this is faithful to the weighted implementation ledger and is internally consistent across manifest/registry/validator/test, so it is not a finding. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found.
